page.title: io-homecontrol
header.title: io-homecontrol
popup.no_favorite_set: No favorite set — use Edit to set one.
popup.repeat_on_no_response: Repeat command if device does not respond
popup.active_blocks_destructive: Disable Active before unpairing or deleting.
popup.identifying: Identifying device — watch for a brief movement.
popup.deactivate_warning: The device will be kept as inactive and can be re-activated later.
popup.delete_warning: Permanent removal. The physical device cannot be re-paired without factory reset.
popup.pair_step1_text: Hold the PROG button on an existing remote until the device (shutter/blind) makes a short movement. Then press Start — the controller will keep scanning for up to 2 minutes.
popup.pair_step2_scanning: Scanning for up to 2 minutes... hold PROG on an existing remote until the device moves, then wait.
popup.pair_step3_link_remote: Do you want to link a remote to this device?
popup.pair_timeout: No device found. Make sure it is in pairing mode.
popup.remote_capture_prompt: Press any button on the remote to register it...
help.device:
Devices are discovered automatically when they respond to a status poll.
Use the edit button to rename a device.
Open / Stop / Close controls the actuator directly.
help.remote:
Step 1: Remotes -> (+)
Create a new remote entry.
Step 2: Enter the remote ID (visible in the log when a button is pressed).
Step 3: Click Edit and link the remote to a device.

status.session-ended: Session ended. Check Graylog for observed frames.
toast.password-too-short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
toast.mqtt-disabled: MQTT disabled. Reboot to fully disconnect.
toast.mqtt-saved-reboot: MQTT settings saved. Reboot to fully disconnect.
toast.controller-saved: Controller settings saved. Reboot to apply.
toast.device-offline: Device did not come back online.
toast.node-address-invalid: Node address must be exactly 6 hex characters.
confirm.restore-backup: Restore this backup? Current settings will be overwritten.
confirm.factory-reset-1: Factory reset will erase all settings and devices. Are you sure?
confirm.factory-reset-2: This cannot be undone. Confirm factory reset?
confirm.wifi-change: The device will restart and connect to '{ssid}'. If credentials are wrong the fallback AP (io-rts-setup) will appear after a short delay.
confirm.network-reboot: Network settings saved. The device needs to reboot to apply changes. Reboot now?
status.press-remote-button: Press any button on the remote…
status.no-remote-detected: No remote detected. Retry or enter ID manually.
status.select-at-least-one-device: Select at least one device to link this remote to.
help.wifi.title: WiFi
help.wifi.what-it-does.label: What it does
help.wifi.what-it-does.body: Connects the device to your home WiFi network. WiFi is required for the web interface, MQTT integration, and over-the-air firmware updates.
help.wifi.after-saving.label: After saving
help.wifi.after-saving.body: The device reboots immediately and attempts to connect. If the credentials are wrong or the network is unreachable, the Fallback AP (io-rts-setup by default) will appear after a short delay.
help.wifi.scan.label: Scan
help.wifi.scan.body: Lists nearby networks. Select one to pre-fill the name. You still need to enter the password.
help.fallback-ap.title: Fallback AP
help.fallback-ap.what-it-does.label: What it does
help.fallback-ap.what-it-does.body: Creates a local WiFi hotspot when the device cannot reach your network. Connect to it to recover access to the settings page.
help.fallback-ap.hotspot-name.label: Hotspot name
help.fallback-ap.hotspot-name.body: The SSID (WiFi network name) for the fallback hotspot. Default: io-rts-setup. Keep it short and identifiable.
help.fallback-ap.password.label: Password
help.fallback-ap.password.body: WPA2 password for the hotspot. Minimum 8 characters. Also used as the password for CLI and web interface access. Leave blank for an open (passwordless) network — not recommended if the device is reachable from outside your home.
help.fallback-ap.retries-boot.label: Retries (boot)
help.fallback-ap.retries-boot.body: Number of WiFi connection attempts at startup before activating the hotspot. Increase this on networks that are slow to assign addresses.
help.fallback-ap.retries-running.label: Retries (running)
help.fallback-ap.retries-running.body: Number of reconnection attempts after a disconnect before activating the hotspot.
help.fallback-ap.timeout.label: Timeout (s)
help.fallback-ap.timeout.body: How long the hotspot stays active before shutting down automatically. Set to 0 to keep it on indefinitely. After the timeout, the device returns to normal WiFi-only mode.
help.network.title: Network / IP
help.network.dhcp.label: DHCP (recommended)
help.network.dhcp.body: The router assigns an IP address automatically. Access the device at http://io-rts-esp32.local (mDNS) or find its IP in your router's client list.
help.network.static-ip.label: Static IP
help.network.static-ip.body: Fixes the device to a specific IP address. Useful when you need a predictable address without relying on mDNS. Always also set Gateway and DNS — without them, cloud imports, time sync, and OTA updates won't work.
help.network.hostname.label: Hostname
help.network.hostname.body: Used for mDNS (http://<hostname>.local) and as the MQTT client ID if not overridden. Must be 1–32 characters, letters, digits and hyphens only.
help.network.sntp-server.label: SNTP server
help.network.sntp-server.body: Time synchronisation server. Default: pool.ntp.org. Required for accurate timestamps in logs and scheduled operations. Use your router's address if internet access is restricted.
help.mqtt.title: MQTT
help.mqtt.what-it-does.label: What it does
help.mqtt.what-it-does.body: Publishes device position and state to an MQTT broker and listens for control commands. Primary integration method for Home Assistant and Node-RED.
help.mqtt.discovery.label: Discovery
help.mqtt.discovery.body: When enabled, announces all devices to Home Assistant using the standard MQTT discovery protocol. Devices appear automatically in HA under the configured discovery prefix (default: homeassistant).
help.mqtt.tls.label: TLS
help.mqtt.tls.body: Use port 8883 for encrypted connections. The broker certificate is validated against the built-in CA bundle. Self-signed certs are not supported.
help.mqtt.topics.label: Topics
help.mqtt.topics.body: Position updates are published to <topic>/<device-id>/state. Commands are received on <topic>/<device-id>/set. The topic root is configurable.
help.syslog.title: Syslog
help.syslog.what-it-does.label: What it does
help.syslog.what-it-does.body: Streams log messages in real time to a remote syslog server over UDP (RFC5424 format). Useful for debugging without a serial connection.
help.syslog.compatible-servers.label: Compatible servers
help.syslog.compatible-servers.body: Works with Graylog, Papertrail, rsyslog, and any RFC5424 receiver. Configure the receiver to listen on the same port.
help.syslog.min-level.label: Min level
help.syslog.min-level.body: Controls log verbosity. 7 = debug (everything), 6 = info, 5 = notice, 4 = warning, 3 = error. Higher values mean less output.
help.somfy.title: Somfy / Overkiz
help.somfy.what-it-does.label: What it does
help.somfy.what-it-does.body: Imports io-homecontrol devices from your Somfy TaHoma / Connexoon cloud account. Saves time compared to adding devices one by one via the Pair button.
help.somfy.what-is-imported.label: What is imported
help.somfy.what-is-imported.body: Only io-homecontrol devices (roller shutters, awnings, blinds) are imported. Other device types (lights, alarms) are skipped.
help.somfy.prerequisite.label: Prerequisite
help.somfy.prerequisite.body: Your devices must already be paired with a TaHoma or Connexoon box and visible in the Somfy app. The import does not pair devices — it copies their identifiers.
help.somfy.credentials.label: Credentials
help.somfy.credentials.body: Uses your Somfy account (email + password). Credentials are sent directly to the Somfy cloud API and are not stored on the device.
help.controller.title: Controller Identity
help.controller.node-address.label: Node address
help.controller.node-address.body: The 6-hex-digit io-homecontrol address of this controller (e.g. A1B1C3). Must be unique on your network. Devices are paired to this specific address — changing it makes existing pairings unusable until you re-pair.
help.controller.tx-power.label: TX power
help.controller.tx-power.body: Radio transmit power in dBm, 0–20. Higher values extend range through walls but may cause interference. Default 17 dBm works for most installations. Changes take effect immediately without reboot.
help.controller.passive-mode.label: Passive mode
help.controller.passive-mode.body: Listens to io-homecontrol traffic without transmitting. Useful for protocol analysis and debugging. In passive mode, no device commands are sent and pairings have no effect.
help.io-key.title: IO System Key
help.io-key.what-it-is.label: What it is
help.io-key.what-it-is.body: A 128-bit AES key shared between this controller and all paired io-homecontrol devices. The key is used to authenticate every command — without the correct key no device will respond.
help.io-key.changing-key.label: ⚠ Changing the key
help.io-key.changing-key.body: Changing this key immediately breaks communication with all currently paired devices. You must re-pair every device after a key change.
help.io-key.learn.label: Learn
help.io-key.learn.body: Receive the key from a TaHoma box or other controller. Put TaHoma in 'share key' mode from its app, then press Learn here. Both controllers will then share the same key and can control the same devices.
help.io-key.send-key.label: Send Key
help.io-key.send-key.body: Push this controller's key to a TaHoma box. Put TaHoma in 'receive key from device' mode from its app, then press Send Key here.
help.io-key.pair.label: Pair
help.io-key.pair.body: Add a new io-homecontrol device (roller shutter, awning, blind) to this controller's network. The device receives the current system key during the pairing sequence.
help.io-key.sniff.label: Sniff
help.io-key.sniff.body: Passively captures the system key from live traffic between a remote control and a device. Press a button on the remote during the capture window.
help.ota-key.title: OTA Key
help.ota-key.what-it-is.label: What it is
help.ota-key.what-it-is.body: An authentication token required for firmware and web UI uploads via HTTP. Prevents unauthorized uploads to the device.
help.ota-key.usage.label: Usage
help.ota-key.usage.body: Pass it as the X-OTA-Key header when POSTing to /api/ota (firmware) or /api/upload/web (web files). Retrieve the current key from /api/ota/key at any time.
help.ota-key.rotation.label: Rotation
help.ota-key.rotation.body: Generate a new random key if the device is accessible from outside your network or if you suspect the key has been exposed.
help.backup.title: Backup / Restore
help.backup.backup.label: Backup
help.backup.backup.body: Downloads a JSON file containing all device definitions, remote links, WiFi credentials, and settings. Does not include the IO system key or OTA key.
help.backup.restore.label: Restore
help.backup.restore.body: Uploads a previously saved backup. Existing devices and settings are replaced. The device reboots after a successful restore.
popup.invert_desc: Swap which end counts as fully open.
popup.device_identify_desc: Triggers a brief movement to locate the device.
popup.deactivate_desc: Keeps device in list but removes controls. Reversible.
status.remote_already_linked: This remote is already linked — editing existing links.
status.link_partial_fail: Some devices could not be linked: {ids}. The remote may be partially linked.
status.remote_id_invalid: Must be exactly 6 hex characters (0–9, A–F).
